name: Business And Industrial
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving individuals and businesses in three Indiana counties with loans, savings accounts, and nationwide ATM access.
description_long:
Business & Industrial Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union chartered to serve individuals and businesses in Bartholomew, Jennings, and Jefferson Counties in Indiana. The credit union has been operating as a community-focused financial institution with the stated mission of being "Member Focused. Community Minded." As a federally chartered credit union, B&I is regulated and insured by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration), providing deposit insurance protection for member accounts.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services designed for both personal and business banking needs. These include various loan products (auto, home, home equity, boat, RV, motorcycle, and signature loans), share savings and share draft (checking) accounts, certificates, IRA accounts, credit cards, and online banking with no fees. Members can access their accounts through new online banking platforms, and the credit union provides loan calculators and skip-a-payment options for flexibility. The institution also offers direct deposit services for payroll, Social Security, VA compensation, and military checks.

What distinguishes B&I Federal Credit Union is its emphasis on member convenience and community engagement. The credit union is a participant in the Credit Union CO-OP Network, providing members access to over 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through CO-OP partner locations including 7-Eleven, Costco, and Walgreens. Members can locate surcharge-free ATMs via text, GPS download, phone, or online portal. The credit union actively engages in community programs, such as April's Credit Union Youth Month featuring youth savings accounts, coloring contests, and gift giveaways. This localized approach combined with national network access reflects their dual focus on personal service and practical member benefits.

B&I Federal Credit Union is a solid choice for residents and business owners in its service area seeking traditional credit union benefits without membership fees. The main limitation is geographic—membership eligibility is restricted to individuals and businesses in the three-county Indiana service area. The credit union's website provides basic information about services, but detailed product rates and terms are not publicly displayed, requiring members to contact the institution directly. This is typical for credit unions but may require more direct communication compared to banks that publish rates online.
services: Direct Deposit (payroll, Social Security, VA, military)
pros: Access to 30,000+ surcharge-free CO-OP Network ATMs nationwide despite local-focus membership model
No fees on online banking platform—specifically advertised as convenient and fee-free
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits return to members, not shareholders
NCUA federal insurance protection on member deposits up to $250,000
Skip-a-payment option allows deferring one consumer loan payment per 12-month rolling period for flexibility
Direct deposit setup available for payroll, Social Security, VA, and military checks
Community engagement programs like Credit Union Youth Month with tangible member benefits (piggy banks, prize drawings)
cons: Membership restricted to Bartholomew, Jennings, and Jefferson Counties in Indiana—geographic limitation excludes most US consumers
No interest rates, term lengths, or APR information published on website; requires direct contact to compare loan and savings products
Limited online presence and digital features compared to larger financial institutions; basic website provides minimal product detail
No mention of business-specific loan programs, SBA loans, or commercial products despite listing "Businesses" in service area
Small institution may have limited loan product customization or faster processing times compared to larger credit unions
best_for: Residents of Bartholomew, Jennings, or Jefferson Counties in Indiana seeking member-owned community banking
Individuals with frequent out-of-state travel wanting surcharge-free nationwide ATM access through CO-OP network
Local business owners in the service area needing a community-focused financial partner
Families seeking youth savings accounts and community-engaged banking with educational programs
diagnosis: Business & Industrial FCU is best for Indiana residents and local business owners in its three-county service area who value member-owned credit union benefits, community engagement, and nationwide ATM access. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility—membership is limited to Bartholomew, Jennings, and Jefferson Counties, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Business and Industrial — NCUA-insured credit union in COLUMBUS, IN. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
